jokes.text: - Wow! So this is a real TV station? - Well, it's a Fox affiliate.
jokes.setup: Fry's excitement about being at a 'real' TV station
jokes.punchline: Leela's deadpan response that it's just a Fox affiliate
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama meta-humor taking a jab at Fox, the network that aired the show. Clean setup/punchline structure with perfect economy.

jokes.text: That's a chick show. I prefer programs of the genre "World's Blankiest Blank."
jokes.setup: Fry dismissing Single Female Lawyer as a 'chick show'
jokes.punchline: His preference for the generic 'World's Blankiest Blank' format
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of reality TV formatting that was becoming popular. Good character moment showing Fry's lowbrow preferences.

jokes.text: - She's in the world's shortiest skirt. - I'm in.
jokes.setup: Bender pointing out the lawyer's short skirt
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately changing his mind about watching
jokes.explanation: Simple male gaze humor. Predictable but efficiently executed and true to Fry's character.

jokes.text: If you care about the case, you should sleep with me.
jokes.setup: The ethical concern about sleeping with a client
jokes.punchline: The twisted logic that caring about the case requires sleeping with her
jokes.explanation: Parodies cheesy legal drama tropes with absurd reverse logic. Good structure but generic character.

jokes.text: - But this is a unisex bathroom! - Overruled, counselor.
jokes.setup: Jenny's protest about the bathroom setting
jokes.punchline: Judge treating bathroom logistics as a legal objection to overrule
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor treating mundane reality with legal formality. Perfect economy and unexpected escalation.

jokes.text: - My God! You knocked Fox off the air. - Like anyone on Earth cares.
jokes.setup: Leela's shock about knocking Fox off the air
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response about Fox's relevance
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama self-deprecating humor about their own network. Biting the hand that feeds with perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Could you repeat that last p-?
jokes.setup: Earlier interrupted transmission line
jokes.punchline: Getting cut off at the same spot again
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure showing the aliens experiencing the same frustration. Simple but effective repetition.

jokes.text: ...and her compellingly short garment!
jokes.setup: Alien demanding to know about the plucky lawyer
jokes.punchline: The formal, alien way of describing a miniskirt
jokes.explanation: Funny alien perspective on human clothing with overly formal language. Good character voice for the aliens.

jokes.text: The phony-baloney holiday crammed down our throats by union gangsters?
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning Labor Day
jokes.punchline: Hermes's over-the-top anti-union rant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment with his bureaucratic paranoia. Strong character integration and voice.

jokes.text: It's HDTV. It has better resolution than the real world.
jokes.setup: Leela saying Fry needs to see the real world
jokes.punchline: Fry's defense that HDTV has better resolution than reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tech-obsessed future logic that actually makes twisted sense. Perfect Fry character moment with great quotability.

jokes.text: - Not covered. - Just get in the car.
jokes.setup: Leela saying everyone's too polite to mention Fry's bed sores
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual correction about degree of coverage
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Fry's oblivious correction making it worse. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: In the 2600s, New Yorkers elected a villainous governor. He stole most of the world's monuments.
jokes.setup: Fry's confusion about world monuments being in New York
jokes.punchline: The casual explanation of electing a villainous governor who stole monuments
jokes.explanation: Great world-building humor that's both absurd and believable for Futurama's universe. Good political satire undertones.

jokes.text: Truly a great man. Look at him up there.
jokes.setup: Just described the governor as villainous
jokes.punchline: Immediately praising him as 'truly a great man'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal showing the Professor's moral flexibility. Clean structure with quick turnaround.

jokes.text: Like my dad used to make, until McDonald's fired him.
jokes.setup: Bender talking about his cooking
jokes.punchline: Revealing his dad was fired from McDonald's
jokes.explanation: Good misdirect with the punchline undercutting any cooking prowess. Efficient structure.

jokes.text: Bite my red-hot glowing ass! Wait a minute. Red-hot glowing ass? I'll be right back.
jokes.setup: Bender's usual catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Realizing his ass is literally red-hot and needs attention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that makes Bender's catchphrase literal and urgent. Great character moment showing his self-awareness.

jokes.text: - Like a cheap French harlot. - French?
jokes.setup: Professor commenting on Amy's appearance
jokes.punchline: Amy only being offended by the 'French' part
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirect with Amy's priorities completely backwards. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: I pretend to steal your girl, you punch me, I go down... she swoons, you slip me 50 bucks.
jokes.setup: Professional beach bully approaching Fry
jokes.punchline: Explaining the elaborate staged routine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of beach bully trope by making it a professional service. Original and well-structured.

jokes.text: $50?! Not even if she was my girlfriend. Take her.
jokes.setup: Beach bully's $50 fee
jokes.punchline: Fry rejecting the deal and abandoning Leela
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Fry's cheapness and social awkwardness. Good escalation from previous setup.

jokes.text: No, thanks, ma'am. I'm actually gay.
jokes.setup: Beach bully offer to walk on beach with Amy
jokes.punchline: Professional beach bully breaking character to decline
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of tough guy stereotype. Clean delivery and unexpected character depth.

jokes.text: You gotta spring me! I won't survive in here! I'm too pretty!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trapped in sand castle
jokes.punchline: Claiming he's 'too pretty' for prison/captivity
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg delusion about his attractiveness. Good character moment but predictable premise.

jokes.text: King Arthur would've lived in it if he were a fiddler crab.
jokes.setup: Professor praising the sandcastle
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific King Arthur/fiddler crab comparison
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Professor logic combining medieval history with beach creatures. Original and memorable.

jokes.text: ...they forced the most intelligent of us to pair off and mate continuously.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining what happened during last alien invasion
jokes.punchline: The 'horrible' fate of continuous mating for the intelligent
jokes.explanation: Good subversion where the 'terrible' alien punishment is actually desirable. Classic sci-fi parody.

jokes.text: Oh, yes.
jokes.setup: Professor's description of forced mating
jokes.punchline: Amy's clearly aroused response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction comedy. Amy's character coming through in minimal dialogue.

jokes.text: Once again, today's winning lotto number was four.
jokes.setup: TV news broadcast during alien attack
jokes.punchline: The absurdly simple lottery number
jokes.explanation: Subtle background absurdity that suggests future lottery corruption or simplification. Good world-building humor.

jokes.text: Get down on all fours and lick boot. Give our alien masters whatever they-
jokes.setup: Presidential address about alien invasion
jokes.punchline: Immediately advocating complete surrender in degrading terms
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about spineless leadership. The vivid imagery makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Is this thing on?
jokes.setup: Lrrr taking over the broadcast
jokes.punchline: Checking the microphone like a nervous public speaker
jokes.explanation: Humanizes the alien conqueror with relatable public speaking anxiety. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: ...fresh from his bloody triumph over the Gandhi Nebula pacifists...
jokes.setup: Introducing military leader Zapp Brannigan
jokes.punchline: His 'triumph' was against pacifists, ironically named after Gandhi
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor contrasting 'bloody triumph' with pacifists. Shows Zapp's incompetence and the absurd nature of his 'victories'.

jokes.text: Look, it's that idiotic windbag you slept with.
jokes.setup: Zapp Brannigan appearing on TV
jokes.punchline: Bender's blunt reference to Leela's past with Zapp
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - tactless but efficient. Good callback to established character history.

jokes.text: - A what? - You know. A coward.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be a conscientious objector
jokes.punchline: His honest clarification that he's just a coward
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender character moment with perfect economy. The brutal honesty is both funny and true to character.

jokes.text: It's every robot's duty to give his life for humanity. Oh, crap.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining robot patriotism circuits being activated
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate regret as his programming kicks in
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Bender's conflict between cowardice and programming. Clean structure.

jokes.text: But I don't care if your skin's red or tan or Chinese.
jokes.setup: Zapp giving inspirational speech about diversity
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Chinese' as a skin color
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment showing his well-meaning but ignorant worldview. Great character writing.

jokes.text: If we hit that bull's-eye... the rest of the dominoes will fall like a house of cards. Checkmate.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining military strategy
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors from different games nonsensically
jokes.explanation: Iconic Zapp line mixing game metaphors with complete confidence. Perfect character voice and became highly quotable.

jokes.text: My strategy is so simple an idiot could have devised it.
jokes.setup: Zapp praising his own strategy
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting his own intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp self-own delivered with complete obliviousness. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Wouldn't it make more sense to send the robots in first-?
jokes.setup: Zapp's suicidal strategy
jokes.punchline: Leela's logical question getting interrupted
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman moment for Leela pointing out obvious tactical sense. Sets up next joke.

jokes.text: When I'm in command, son, every mission's a suicide mission.
jokes.setup: Context of military planning
jokes.punchline: Zapp's proud admission of his lethal incompetence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that perfectly encapsulates Zapp's lethal leadership style. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: We should wait till after, in case neither of us comes back.
jokes.setup: Zapp suggesting they make love before battle
jokes.punchline: Leela's logical but morbid counter-proposal
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Leela's practical approach to pre-battle romance. The logic is twisted but sound.

jokes.text: I'll be a science-fiction hero, like Uhura or Captain Janeway or Xena.
jokes.setup: Leela preparing for battle
jokes.punchline: Mixing actual sci-fi characters with Xena
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture humor mixing genres. Shows Leela's aspirations but isn't particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Sure. I just like TV better.
jokes.setup: Leela asking if Fry can tell TV from real life
jokes.punchline: Fry's simple preference for television
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier TV obsession theme. Perfect Fry character moment with honest simplicity.

jokes.text: - What is that? - It appears to be the mother ship. - Then what did we just blow up? - The Hubble Telescope.
jokes.setup: Celebrating victory over the mother ship
jokes.punchline: Revealing they destroyed the Hubble Telescope instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirect showing military incompetence. Perfect structure with escalating absurdity.

jokes.text: Stop exploding, you cowards!
jokes.setup: Ships being destroyed in battle
jokes.punchline: Zapp commanding his troops to stop dying as if it's cowardice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Zapp line treating death as a character flaw. Perfect economy and became iconic for the character.

jokes.text: Sorry. Check back in three days. A week at the most.
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating that the Professor survived
jokes.punchline: Professor casually giving his remaining lifespan
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character moment treating his mortality with scientific precision and dark humor.

jokes.text: - Miniskirts? That sounds familiar. - Really?
jokes.setup: Aliens describing Single Female Lawyer's miniskirts
jokes.punchline: Fry's TV knowledge making him useful and Lrrr's interest
jokes.explanation: Sets up Fry's TV expertise becoming valuable. Straightforward but necessary plot development.

jokes.text: - Wait, I know her. - You do not, you big fat liar.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming to know Jenny McNeal
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's childish insult response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lrrr's childish side despite being a conqueror. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: - You see- - Magic. Got it.
jokes.setup: Professor starting to explain electromagnetic waves
jokes.punchline: Fry dismissing science as magic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment showing his anti-intellectual stance. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Beer. I would think.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Coke was spilled on the transmitter
jokes.punchline: Bender's correction based on his drinking preferences
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment inserting his alcoholism into the conversation. Efficient and true to character.

jokes.text: Most videotapes from that era were damaged in 2443... during the second coming of Jesus.
jokes.setup: Explaining why no tape copies exist
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Jesus's second coming as historical fact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway world-building line treating religious prophecy as mundane historical event. Subversive and memorable.

jokes.text: Let's put on a show!
jokes.setup: Everyone offering their skills to recreate the TV show
jokes.punchline: The classic Mickey Rooney/Judy Garland movie reference
jokes.explanation: Standard reference to old movie trope. Works for the situation but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Plus, you don't have the thighs for a miniskirt.
jokes.setup: Leela declining to star as Jenny McNeal
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt assessment of Leela's legs
jokes.explanation: Good Amy character moment with her tactless honesty. Straightforward insult humor but well-delivered.

jokes.text: If she wants to be taken seriously, why not tear the judge's head off?
jokes.setup: Watching the courtroom drama unfold
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's violent solution to legal problems
jokes.explanation: Great alien perspective on human legal systems. Shows Lrrr's violent worldview applied to mundane situations.

jokes.text: It is true what they say. Women are from Persei Seven. Men are from Persei Nine.
jokes.setup: Context of relationship drama in the show
jokes.punchline: Alien version of 'Men are from Mars, Women are from Venus'
jokes.explanation: Clever alien adaptation of the famous relationship book title. Good world-building and cultural commentary.

jokes.text: It took an hour to write. I thought it'd take an hour to read.
jokes.setup: Only having two pages of dialogue for the show
jokes.punchline: Fry's logical but naive assumption about time correlation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - simple, wrong, but understandable. Shows his inexperience with creative work.

jokes.text: Clever things make people feel stupid. Unexpected things make them feel scared.
jokes.setup: Amy defending the marriage twist as clever and unexpected
jokes.punchline: Fry's cynical but accurate analysis of TV audience psychology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on television writing and audience expectations. Incisive cultural criticism delivered by the show's most naive character.

jokes.text: Deep down I knew it might one day help me save the world.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining his TV expertise
jokes.punchline: Claiming he always knew TV watching would save the world
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Fry retroactively justifying his laziness. Relatable rationalization humor.

jokes.text: Overall, I would rate it a C-Plus. Okay, not great.
jokes.setup: Aliens being satisfied with the performance
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's harsh but fair critical assessment
jokes.explanation: Good humor in the alien's honest critical assessment. Treats world-saving performance like a school assignment.

jokes.text: Way to overact, Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: The dramatic performance they just gave
jokes.punchline: Leela criticizing Zoidberg's acting
jokes.explanation: Simple character interaction. Shows the group dynamic but isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Now we must return to our planet... to catch a 1000-year-old Leno monologue.
jokes.setup: Aliens preparing to leave
jokes.punchline: Their next priority is watching ancient late-night TV
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the time-delay premise and shows aliens' entertainment priorities. Solid world-building humor.

jokes.text: At the end of the episode, everything is right back to normal.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining the secret of all TV shows
jokes.punchline: The reset button nature of episodic television
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on TV storytelling conventions. Self-aware ending that comments on its own structure.
